article_title: Embark Studios Replaces AI Voice Lines in Arc Raiders With Human Actors After Player Backlash, CEO Admits Professionals Are 'Better Than AI'

content_preview.title: Embark Studios Replaces AI Voice Lines in Arc Raiders With Human Actors After Player Backlash, CEO Admits Professionals Are 'Better Than AI'
content_preview.summary: After selling 14 million copies, Embark Studios is replacing AI-generated voice lines in Arc Raiders with performances by human actors. CEO Patrick Söderlund acknowledged a clear quality gap, though the studio will retain text-to-speech for non-essential audio.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Embark Studios has replaced a significant portion of the AI-generated voice lines in its cooperative shooter Arc Raiders with dialogue recorded by professional human actors, CEO Patrick Söderlund confirmed in mid-March. The reversal follows months of player criticism over the game's use of text-to-speech technology and comes after the title [sold 14 million copies](https://www.engadget.com/gaming/arc-raiders-replaced-some-of-its-ai-generated-voice-lines-with-professional-actors-184915627.html) s...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written News piece covering a timely AI-in-gaming story. Good structure with clear sections on the reversal, partial retention, and industry context.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources fetched. Kotaku confirms Söderlund's exact quote ('A real professional actor is better than AI'), TTS retention for pings, and actor licensing. Engadget confirms 500K peak concurrent Steam users, October 2025 launch, quarter-AAA budget, and the same CEO quote. PC Gamer page returned mostly CSS but metadata confirms the article exists. Note: the '14 million copies' sales figure attributed to Engadget was not found in the extracted content — it may exist in the full article but could not be independently verified from the fetch.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core narrative and quotes verified across sources. The 14 million sales figure is the only claim that could not be independently confirmed from the fetched content, though it may be in portions not captured by extraction.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Balanced coverage presenting both the studio's perspective and industry critics' views.
editor_notes.originality: No existing Arc Raiders or Embark Studios coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong News article with verified quotes and well-balanced coverage of a significant AI-in-gaming story. Approved for publication.
